PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-15-2013 Continued To Oct-28-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Schmuck for Jeffrey McDonnell. Edward Koplowitz for and with Joyce McDonnell.

Jeffrey McDonnell was present at the commencement of court but left prior to his matter being called. Mr. Koplowitz stated that they have a stipulation. Mr. Schmuck represented to the Court that he has full authority to enter into the stipulation, stated that his Client understands and approves the stipulation, and that his Client had to leave to catch a flight. The stipulation was read in open Court as follows: 1) The Petition for Probate of Will is continued for 90-days. 2) With regard to the outstanding subpoena to James Berge, records will be produced pursuant to the subpoena and the deposition of Mr. Berge will be taken within 45-days. 3) No other discovery will be propounded. 4) The house located at 300 Laidley Street will be sold. They will go forward with a 90-day listing of the property with McGuire Realty. Mr. Koplowitz will keep Mr. Schmuck advised. If the property is sold, the proceeds will go into a joint Attorney Trust account or a blocked account. 5) The Parties will go to mediation in Santa Clara County with Judge Catherine Gallagher or another agreed upon Judge before the next hearing. Said stipulation is to be prepared, signed and filed by July 29, 2013. Status on filing of the stipulation was set for July 29, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for Probate of Will was continued to October 28, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)
